<!-- AUTOGENERATED:START - Do not edit between these markers -->
# teams app user-auth oauth add

Add an OAuth connection to an Azure bot

## Usage

```
teams app user-auth oauth add [appId] [options]
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `appId` | App ID | No |

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--provider <name>` | Service provider (e.g., Aadv2, GitHub, Google) |
| `--connection-name <name>` | OAuth connection name |
| `--client-id <id>` | Provider client ID |
| `--client-secret <secret>` | Provider client secret |
| `--scopes <scopes>` | Provider scopes (space-delimited) |
| `--parameters <params>` | [OPTIONAL] Extra provider params (key=value key=value) |
<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->

## Details

Adds an OAuth connection setting to an Azure bot. Requires the bot to be in Azure.

### Interactive Mode

Without flags, the CLI fetches the provider list via `az bot authsetting list-providers` and shows a searchable picker. Then prompts for connection name, client ID, secret, and scopes.

### Common Providers

- `Aadv2` — Azure AD v2 (for SSO, use `app user-auth sso setup` instead)
- `GitHub` — GitHub OAuth
- `Google` — Google OAuth

### Extra Parameters

Some providers need additional parameters beyond client ID/secret/scopes. Pass them as space-separated key=value pairs:

```bash
--parameters "tenantId=common domain_hint=contoso.com"
```

### Examples

```bash
teams app user-auth oauth add <appId> \
  --provider GitHub \
  --connection-name github-login \
  --client-id <github-client-id> \
  --client-secret <github-secret> \
  --scopes "read:user user:email"
```

### Prerequisites

- Azure CLI (`az`) installed and logged in
- Bot must be in Azure (check with `teams app bot status`)
